name: Wattakka (Curried Pumpkin)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the pumpkin and cut off one-quarter of the top.
2. Scoop out the flesh to within 2 cm of the shell, then cube it. Set the shell aside for later use.
3. Slice the onion and chilies, and crush the garlic.
4. Heat the oil in a pan and fry the onion and chilies with curry leaves until they are soft.
5. Add the cubed pumpkin, salt, pepper, turmeric, and thin coconut milk to the pan. Cook until the pumpkin is cooked through.
6. Pour the thick coconut milk into the mixture containing ground rice, mustard, and garlic. Stir as it thickens.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer for a few minutes.
8. Remove from heat and spoon the contents into the pumpkin shell.
9. Sprinkle with curry powder and lime juice.
